 {"id":"bd-3dqbk","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Restart Pane","description":"E2E tests for Robot Restart Pane with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Soft Exit Success
1. Spawn test session with claude agent
2. Verify agent is running
3. Call --robot-restart-pane
4. Verify exit_method is 'soft'
5. Verify new agent is running

### Scenario 2: Hard Kill Required
1. Spawn test session
2. Make agent unresponsive (e.g., infinite loop)
3. Call --robot-restart-pane
4. Verify exit_method is 'kill'
5. Verify new agent is running

### Scenario 3: With Bead Assignment
1. Spawn test session
2. Create test bead
3. Call --robot-restart-pane --bead=<test-bead>
4. Verify bead_assigned in response
5. Verify prompt appears in pane output

### Scenario 4: Invalid Pane
1. Call --robot-restart-pane with non-existent pane
2. Verify error response with available panes

### Scenario 5: Timing Verification
1. Instrument timing in restart sequence
2. Verify 100ms gap between Ctrl-Cs
3. Verify total time matches expectations

## Test Infrastructure
ntm spawn test-restart --cc=1 --no-user
# ... run tests ...
ntm kill test-restart","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:26:03.248632534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:26:54.409475790Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dqbk","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:26:03.277436257Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3dqbk","depends_on_id":"bd-ojx0p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:26:54.409453798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3dxk","title":"EPIC: End-of-Session Output Summarization","description":"**IDEA #13 - PROGRESS CLARITY (72% confidence)**

## What It Does
When ending a session, automatically generate a summary of what each agent accomplished, decisions made, and work remaining.

## Why This Matters
- Currently: Users must mentally track what each agent did
- After: Clear record of session accomplishments
- Valuable for handoffs, standup reports, documentation

## Commands
ntm summarize myproject
ntm kill myproject --summarize

## Summary Generation Process
1. Capture full scrollback from all panes
2. Extract key accomplishments (file changes, commits, test results)
3. Generate per-agent summaries
4. Compile into session report

## Example Output
# Session Summary: myproject
Generated: 2026-01-19 15:30:00 UTC
Duration: 2h 15m

## Agent Accomplishments

### cc_1 (Claude/Opus)
**Tasks Completed:**
- Implemented JWT authentication in internal/auth/jwt.go
- Added middleware for token validation
- Created 12 unit tests (all passing)

**Key Decisions:**
- Used RS256 for JWT signing
- Set 1-hour token expiry

**Files Modified:**
- internal/auth/jwt.go (+245 lines)
- internal/middleware/auth.go (+89 lines)

### cod_1 (Codex)
**Tasks Completed:**
- Fixed race condition in cache module (bd-47)

## Session Statistics
- Total commits: 4
- Tests run: 47 (45 passed)
- Estimated cost: $8.50

## Remaining Work
- [ ] bd-45: Integration tests for auth

## Flywheel Alignment
- Creates accountability for agent work
- Helps identify productive vs unproductive agents
- Integrates with BV for task status

## Technical Implementation
- GenerateSessionSummary() analyzing pane output
- extractAccomplishments() using pattern matching
- extractDecisions() identifying key choices
- Integration with git diff for concrete changes

## Dependencies
Depends on: Output Archive (for reliable capture)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:27:30.387608415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:46.822809747Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3dxk","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:06:46.822768650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ejl","title":"Add ntm send --base-prompt flag - prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically","description":"## Overview
Add --base-prompt flag to prepend common instructions to all prompts automatically.

## Problem
Users often want to include standard instructions (coding standards, project context, safety rules) with every prompt but don't want to repeat them.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Always run tests after changes" --msg "Implement feature X"`

The actual prompt sent becomes:
```
Always run tests after changes

Implement feature X
```

## Configuration
Can also be set in ntm.yaml:
```yaml
send:
  base_prompt: |
    Follow AGENTS.md rules.
    Run ubs before committing.
    Use br to track work.
```

## Implementation
```go
func buildPrompt(basePrompt, userPrompt string) string {
    if basePrompt == "" {
        return userPrompt
    }
    return basePrompt + "\n\n" + userPrompt
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Inline base prompt
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Run tests" --msg "Add feature"

# From file
ntm send myproject --base-prompt-file context.md --msg "Add feature"

# With batch (prepended to each)
ntm send myproject --base-prompt "Be careful" --batch prompts.txt
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Base prompt prepended to all messages
- [ ] Works with --msg and --batch
- [ ] Config file support
- [ ] File-based base prompt (--base-prompt-file)
- [ ] Empty base prompt = no-op
- [ ] Clear separator between base and user prompt

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Prompt concatenation
- Unit test: Config file loading
- E2E test: Base prompt with batch send","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

## Test Coverage

### tmux Detection Tests
- Test binary path detection (/usr/bin/tmux vs alias)
- Test version parsing from various formats
- Test shell alias detection (zsh, bash)
- Test plugin detection (oh-my-zsh tmux plugin)

### Session Structure Tests
- Test window index detection
- Test pane index range parsing
- Test control vs agent pane identification
- Test empty session handling

### Shell Environment Tests
- Test shell type detection (zsh, bash, fish)
- Test oh-my-zsh detection
- Test config path discovery
- Test plugin enumeration

### Timing Tests
- Test default values returned
- Test config file override
- Test environment variable override
- Test priority order (config > env > default)

### Targeting Tests
- Test example generation correctness
- Test format string accuracy
- Test various session names (with special chars)

## Mocking Strategy

- Mock which/type commands
- Mock tmux list-* output
- Mock file system reads for config detection
- Mock environment variables

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Mock configuration at test start
- Command outputs being simulated
- Expected vs actual values

 {"id":"bd-3exkg","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Probe","description":"E2E tests for Robot Probe with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Responsive Agent (keystroke_echo)
```bash
# Setup
ntm spawn test-probe --cc=1
sleep 10  # Wait for agent initialization

# Execute
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2)

# Verify
echo "$result" | jq '.responsive' | grep true
echo "$result" | jq '.confidence' | grep -E 'high|medium'
echo "$result" | jq '.recommendation' | grep healthy
```

### Scenario 2: Unresponsive Pane (shell prompt)
```bash
# Setup: Create session with just shell (no agent)
ntm spawn test-probe-shell --cc=0

# Execute probe on shell prompt
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe-shell 1)

# Shell should still be responsive
echo "$result" | jq '.responsive' | grep true
```

### Scenario 3: Hung Process Simulation
```bash
# Setup: Spawn session, then make pane unresponsive
ntm spawn test-hung --cc=1
# Use a script to create infinite loop in pane
/usr/bin/tmux send-keys -t "test-hung:1.2" "while true; do :; done" Enter

# Execute probe
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-hung 2)

# Should detect unresponsive
echo "$result" | jq '.responsive' | grep false
echo "$result" | jq '.recommendation' | grep -E 'stuck'
```

### Scenario 4: Method Comparison
```bash
# Test keystroke_echo
result1=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2 --method=keystroke_echo)
echo "keystroke_echo: $(echo "$result1" | jq -c '{responsive, latency_ms: .probe_details.latency_ms}')"

# Test interrupt_test (more aggressive)
result2=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2 --method=interrupt_test)
echo "interrupt_test: $(echo "$result2" | jq -c '{responsive, method: .probe_method}')"
```

### Scenario 5: Custom Timeout
```bash
# Short timeout (100ms)
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2 --timeout=100)
echo "Short timeout: $(echo "$result" | jq '.responsive')"

# Long timeout (10s)
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2 --timeout=10000)
echo "Long timeout: $(echo "$result" | jq '.responsive')"
```

### Scenario 6: Aggressive Mode Fallback
```bash
# Test aggressive mode (falls back to interrupt_test if keystroke_echo fails)
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2 --aggressive)
echo "$result" | jq '.probe_method'  # May show fallback
```

### Scenario 7: Invalid Pane
```bash
result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 999)
echo "$result" | jq '.error' | grep true
echo "$result" | jq '.error_code' | grep PANE_NOT_FOUND
```

### Scenario 8: Integration with Diagnose
```bash
# Run probe, then diagnose, verify consistency
probe_result=$(ntm --robot-probe test-probe 2)
diagnose_result=$(ntm --robot-diagnose test-probe --pane 2)

# If probe says unresponsive, diagnose should reflect this
probe_responsive=$(echo "$probe_result" | jq '.responsive')
diagnose_stuck=$(echo "$diagnose_result" | jq '.panes[0].issues[] | select(.issue | contains("stuck"))')
```

## Logging Requirements

Each test scenario must log:
```json
{
  "scenario": "responsive_agent",
  "setup": {"session": "test-probe", "pane": 2, "agent_type": "claude"},
  "probe_params": {"method": "keystroke_echo", "timeout": 5000},
  "result": {"responsive": true, "confidence": "high", "latency_ms": 45},
  "assertions": [{"field": "responsive", "expected": true, "actual": true, "passed": true}],
  "passed": true,
  "duration_ms": 5234
}
```

## Cleanup

```bash
# Cleanup all test sessions
ntm kill test-probe
ntm kill test-probe-shell
ntm kill test-hung
```

## Test Infrastructure

- Requires tmux installed
- Requires ntm with robot mode
- Tests should be idempotent
- Each scenario handles its own setup/teardown","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:45.110817161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:45:41.090994104Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cu1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:33:45.139006256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3exkg","depends_on_id":"bd-43oxm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:34:04.952726663Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3f53","title":"Task: Implement getMemoryContext in spawn","description":"## Purpose
Add function to retrieve and format CM memories during agent spawn.

## Function Signature
func (s *SpawnCmd) getMemoryContext() string

## Logic
1. Check if memory integration enabled in config
2. Create CM client and verify availability
3. Query cm context with project name and task
4. Format response into injectable markdown

## Output Format
# Project Memory from Past Sessions

## Key Rules for This Project
- [b-8f3a2c] Always use structured logging with log/slog
- [b-4e1d7b] Database migrations must be idempotent

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- [b-7d3e8c] Don't add backwards-compatibility shims

## Acceptance Criteria
- Memories retrieved and formatted
- Rule IDs included for agent reference
- Empty string returned when unavailable
- Respects max_rules config limit

## Technical Notes
Call during buildRecoveryContext","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:36:28.547101630Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:51:28.568110942Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:51:28.567944889Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3f53","depends_on_id":"bd-2n9s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3ffb","title":"Add ntm send --dry-run to preview what would be sent to each agent without sending","description":"## Overview
Add --dry-run flag to ntm send to preview what would be sent to each agent without actually sending.

## Problem
Users want to verify prompt targeting before committing to sends, especially with complex targeting rules.

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --dry-run` shows exactly what would be sent without sending.

## Output Format
### Human Mode
```
Dry Run: ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt

Would send 5 prompts:
  1. cc_1: "Implement feature A" (from line 1)
  2. cc_2: "Implement feature B" (from line 5)
  3. cod_1: "Write tests for A" (from line 9)
  4. cod_2: "Write tests for B" (from line 13)
  5. gmi_1: "Review documentation" (from line 17)

Use without --dry-run to execute.
```

### Robot Mode
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "would_send": [
    {"pane": 1, "agent": "cc_1", "prompt": "Implement...", "source": "line:1"},
    {"pane": 2, "agent": "cc_2", "prompt": "Implement...", "source": "line:5"}
  ],
  "total": 5
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Preview batch send
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --dry-run

# Preview with targeting
ntm send myproject --msg "Review code" --type cc --dry-run

# Preview assign
ntm assign myproject --dry-run
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No prompts actually sent in dry-run
- [ ] Output shows exact targeting
- [ ] Works with all send variants
- [ ] Robot mode JSON output
- [ ] Shows source (line number, bead ID)

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Dry-run flag prevents sending
- Unit test: Output format correctness
- E2E test: Dry-run matches actual send targeting","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
